title: Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple
tagline: Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

heroImage.alt: Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple, Kerala

overview.short: Thirumangalam Sree Maha Deva Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu and Shiva at Engandiyur of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India.
overview.description: Thirumangalam Sree Maha Deva Siva Temple is an ancient Hindu temple dedicated to Vishnu and Shiva at Engandiyur of Thrissur District in Kerala state in India. Governed by Kattumadam Devaswam Trust. The presiding deity of the temple are Maha Vishnu and Shiva, located in separate sanctum sanatoriums, facing East. According to folklore, Shiva Linga is swayambhu and sage Parashurama has installed the idol.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Adat (Thrissur)
topPlaces.description: Adat is a town in the Thrissur taluk of Thrissur district with an area of 6.91 km2, located 9 km from the district headquarters of Thrissur and 290 km from the state headquarters of Thiruvananthapuram. It has 1376 households with a total population of 5721 as per the 2011 Census, with 2862 males and 2859 females.

topPlaces.name: Engandiyur
topPlaces.description: Engandiyur is a village in the Chavakkad taluka of the Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. It has an area of 1411 hectares and harbours 5,760 households with a total population of 23,101 as per the 2011 Census.

topPlaces.name: William Fort
topPlaces.description: William Fort or locally known as Chettuva Fort is located in Chettuva, Thrissur District of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Chavakkad
topPlaces.description: Chavakkad, formerly Chowghat, is a municipality in Thrissur district of the Indian state of Kerala. Chavakkad is known for its beach and fishing.

topPlaces.name: Venkitangu
topPlaces.description: Venkitangu is a Panchayath in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. Venkitangu is a village located in Mullassery Block in the Thrissur District of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Mullassery
topPlaces.description: Mullassery is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. It is located 20 km (12 mi) west of District Headquarters Thrissur and 278 km (173 mi) from the state capital Thiruvanathapuram.

topPlaces.name: Manaloor
topPlaces.description: Manalur is a small village in Thrissur district of Kerala state, south India. It is one of the constituencies in Thrissur district.

topPlaces.name: Irimbranallur
topPlaces.description: Irimbranallur is a village in Thrissur district in the state of Kerala, India. It covers an area of 6.46 square kilometres (2.49 sq mi) and has population of 3,200 (2011 census: males 1,406, females 1,794).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (54 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (65 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Thirumangalam Sree Maha Vishnu Siva Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Adat (Thrissur), Engandiyur, William Fort, Chavakkad, Venkitangu.
